mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following rate cuts show no statistical significance above baseline (p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to generate positive 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month trend signals supply-side bottlenecks and weakening economic output, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production contractions during recovery phases yield positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances, or the signal reverses direction during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stagnant or rising federal funds rate indicates real rate tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising inflation and stable or rising rates fails to predict negative returns, or positive returns dominate in > 55% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and corporate revenue potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) or positive returns occur in < 45% of high-growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5% fails to produce positive 5-day returns in > 50% of observations, or the signal loses significance when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment indicates a synchronized supply-demand recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases month-over-month while UNRATE declines
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails during stagflationary regimes where supply growth coincides with rising unemployment.
